content: DSM-5-TR criteria: ≥2 symptoms for ≥1 month (or less if successfully treated), with at least one being delusions, hallucinations, or disorganized speech; other symptoms include grossly disorganized/catatonic behavior or negative symptoms. Continuous signs persist for ≥6 months including ≥1 month of active symptoms plus prodromal/residual periods. Significant functional impairment in work, relationships, or self-care. Schizoaffective and mood disorders ruled out. Substance/medical causes excluded. Specifiers include first episode vs multiple episodes, acute episode status, severity, and presence of catatonia. Course specifiers: episodic with/without residual symptoms, or continuous.
